rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Similar issues to the Wolfe 2004 Trust re "Adjustment to Carrying Value." No receipts have been filed for the distributions to Tina High, but she did get notice and has not objected. (It would have been helpful if the additional property received from the Bruce F. Wolfe 2004 Trust had been shown in the same order as shown on Schedule E of the Bruce F. Wolfe 2004 Trust. In any event, the total amount is the same.) There does not appear to be an order approving the last accounting (for the period 3/7/15 - 3/65/18) for this QTIP Trust. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BRUCE F. WOLFE 2004 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Paragraphs 23 and following refer to this as the QTIP Trust Additional Property Received (p. 3 of the accounting) shows "adjustments" to the carrying values that are not supported. Furthermore, there are references to "inventory" values when there was no inventory, and the "Value per Inventory" of the Dispatch Printing Co is shown as $16,027,765.00, when the property on hand as of 3/7/18 showed the value of those 16 units as $397,280.00. The number of units of each holding is not shown on that schedule of "Additional Property Received". It is unclear how the carry values of the closely held corporations shown on Schedule F (Property on Hand at Close of Account) were determined. There are payments to Niman & Associates, Inc., for accounting fees, but the descriptions do not indicate the period covered by such payments. Two appear to be duplicates. Tax due to late filing with interest in the amount of $1,468.55 was disbursed on 4/23/18 without any explanation. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE OWYANG REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 26, 2000 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; For Accounting; To Appoint Successor Trustee; For Surcharge; For Imposition Of Constructive Trust; And For Emotional Damages
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine how best to proceed. No courtesy copy of either the "supplemental declaration" filed on 6/2/21 or the objections filed on 6/28/21 has been received yet by the Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] A search of this Court's register of actions turned up an earlier case that was opened for this trust: PTR-14-298278. The Court may wish to consolidate the cases. The Court cannot find and order what is not set forth in a verified petition and specifically prayed. A quick skim of the petitioner's attorney's "supplemental declaration" filed on 6/2/21 does not show Ms. Lassahn's consent to serve as successor trustee attached to that pleading and the register of actions does not show a consent filed separately. (Supplements or amendments to a pleading should stand on their own and not be a "response" to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed for the record. Examiners merely raise issues to be addressed in a verified pleading, not answered to the Examiner.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner's notes. a. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. b. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] c. If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.] d. Please explain and file receipts in support of entry 10/12/2020 Reimbursement for Pre-Sale House Expenses of $10,703.89. e. The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/21. Issues necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice to the FTB of this hearing has been filed yet. The FTB filed a Request for Special Notice on 1/21/2020. No petition has been filed yet for a successor administrator to be appointed for this estate. [Probate Code Section 8522(b).] If none of the other surviving nieces or nephews petitions to be appointed as successor administrator and the now deceased administrator's husband does not petition to be appointed as successor administrator, the Court may appoint the Public Administrator to complete estate administration. [Probate Code Section 8461.] (How are grandnephews successors-in-interest to the now deceased administrator? Who were their predeceased parents who were closer kin of the decedent? [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] If the now deceased administrator's husband has been appointed the administrator of her estate, then other successors-in-interest do not need to be identified; however, certified Letters (or the equivalent thereof, as issued by the appropriate court in New Hampshire) must be filed prior to final distribution. [LRSF 14.36 G.]) Supplements or amendments to a pleading should stand on their own and not respond to or "answer" issues raised by an Examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL WESTERBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021. 1) Summary of Account on page 2 does not indicate the account period. Petition on page 1 alleges this account period is 03/05/2020 to 04/06/2021. Account must begin with Decedent's date of death. Matter continued for counsel to amend the petition and re-notice. 2) See LRSF 14.35: Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three-year period must be filed separately. If all interested parties waive an account by the personal representative, the waiver applies to the entire estate administration, not just to a periodic report on the status of administration. 3) Petitioner was appointed with $465,000.00 bond. See LRSF 14.16: Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond. 1) See CRC 7.204. When the bond of a fiduciary must be increased, the Court favors filing of an additional bond rather than a substitute bond. 2) An account will not be approved until the additional bond is on file. 3) Where assets will be coming into or passing through the hands of the fiduciary so as to require an increase of bond, the fiduciary must set forth the information necessary to enable the Court to determine the amount of the increase. 4) Please file timesheets for the ordinary statutory attorney services provided to the personal representative so that the Court may determine whether the requested extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10811(a)). Please provide a legal basis for the request for extraordinary fees. 5) Please file a copy of the Seller's Statement for the sale of the real property. 6) See LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b. The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. Do not put Examiner's name on the proposed order. 7) See LRSF 14.35 D: The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTYE M. MCNAULTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Original financial account statements (or original letters signed by an officer of the financial institution having the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance) must be filed to verify the ending balance of cash in an accounting. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.35 D.] The math in paragraph 22 of the petition is off - shorting the administrator. (Probate Referee Isidoor Bornstein's commission was $180.30, not $180.00.) The E-filing costs were not itemized, showing what was filed on which date, etc., to arrive at $25.00. The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. (Notice to the CVCB is required under Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b), not (a). Rev. and Tax. C. Sect. 19513 was repealed.) If the administrator wishes to be reimbursed the full amount of costs she advanced, an amendment must be filed. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/26/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 2018 JESUS VICINTAYNER ESPINOZA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust; To Determine Property That Belongs To The Estate; And To Determine The Validity Of A Contract To Make A Devise
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to take a first look at the pleadings and address the threshhold issue of venue. the 16061.7 says the principal place of administration is 355 Naples, SF, but neither trustee resides there. the court will want some evidence that administration of the estate actually takes place in that residence (copies of bank statements mailed to that address, cancelled checks showing that address for the trustees, tax statements and/or deeds mailed to that address, etc.). Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

Status of closing the estate is set for 7/26/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 7/26/2022. Appearance will be required on 7/26/2022 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021. 1) No filed response to prior examiner notes. 2) Examiner received an unfiled courtesy copy of an amended petition and other unfiled documents all of which have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEPHEN L. PAUTENIS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: continued to august 16, 2021. notice was mailed on DE-120 but as of 1/1/21, DE-115 was a mandatory form of notice for a petition brought under PC 850. in addition, the siblings are not trust beneficiaries, so mailed notice is insufficient. please review PC 851(b) for the type of service required for persons other than trust beneficiaries, whose interests may be affected by the petition. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORA E WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] The petition does not state the date of death of the decedent's son Patrick Wong. The two individuals identified as his "issue" are not clearly identified with their relationship to the decedent; they are presumably her grandchildren, but they could be more remote issue. 2) The date of death must be specified for all deceased heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D.1.] The date of death of the decedent's spouse, Ki Hong Wong, is not set forth in item 8 of the petition. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE MAE RUSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021 for same issues. Matter may go on for hearing for the sole purpose of taking the matter off calendar for failure to respond to examiner notes. 1) The petition must be amended to request appointment of a person as personal representative not a law firm. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2) Notice does not indicate Lost Will. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. 3) Must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Please amend attachment 3f(3) as it must address the factors and presumption in Probate Code 6124. 6) Please provide legal authority for petitioner having priority as personal representative. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to the notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIGONG HUANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021. 1) See CRC 7.53: An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. 2) Petitioner must select a box on page 3 under #5.a. (3) registered domestic partner or (4) no registered domestic partner. 3) Please explain why petitioner checked on page 2 under #3.g.(2)(b) when there is no nomination of Shihan Li attached to the petition. 4) Please explain why petitioner's address is different on petition page 4 and the Duties and Liabilities. Please confirm petitioner's address. 5) See LRSF 14.18 E: Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WOON BING CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice of this hearing. Item 5a(2) of the petition is incomplete. Item 9 does not show the relationship to the decedent of Amy Chan. A copy of the bank statements must be filed to show how title was vested. (Attachment 7 does not clearly set forth why the accounts in the decedent's name are community property.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (e.g., a verified supplement and the proof of service) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/21/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARILAN A. CONRAD REVOCABLE TRUST DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/16/21. notice of hearing was sent on judicial council form DE-120 but as of 1/1/20, DE-115 is a mandatory form of notice for petitions brought under PC 850. new notice must be mailed on the mandatory form and POS filed no later than noon on 8/5/21. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QI NAN XU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] No proof of publication. A courtesy copy of the proof of publication and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/21/21. Has the petitioner been advised of other options other than formal probate? [Probate Code Section 13500 et seq.] See CRC 7.51(d) re notice to a minor. A guardianship may need to be established for the minor heirs if the decedent's assets consisted of separate property. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN H ROCKWELL III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/2021. 1) Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. 2) See LRSF 14.18 C. 3): When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Must file proof of publication. 5) Please explain why petitioner has a NC Driver's license if she lives in CA. Please explain if petitioner is a resident or non-resident of CA. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
